title: Ashburn, Virginia

infobox.value: is an [unincorporated settlement](/wiki/Unincorporated_area#United_States "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) in [Loudoun County, Virginia](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia"), United States. At the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), its population was 46,349,[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2020-3) up from 3,393 in 1990. It is 30 miles (48 km) northwest of Washington, D.C., and part of the [Washington metropolitan area](/wiki/Washington_metropolitan_area "Washington metropolitan area"). Ashburn is a major hub for [Internet traffic](/wiki/Internet_traffic "Internet traffic") due to its many [data centers](/wiki/Data_center "Data center").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

**Ashburn** is an [unincorporated settlement](/wiki/Unincorporated_area#United_States "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) in [Loudoun County, Virginia](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia"), United States. At the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), its population was 46,349,[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2020-3) up from 3,393 in 1990. It is 30 miles (48 km) northwest of Washington, D.C., and part of the [Washington metropolitan area](/wiki/Washington_metropolitan_area "Washington metropolitan area"). Ashburn is a major hub for [Internet traffic](/wiki/Internet_traffic "Internet traffic") due to its many [data centers](/wiki/Data_center "Data center").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/2018-10-25_14_33_59_View_west_along_Virginia_State_Route_267_%28Dulles_Greenway%29_at_Exit_5_%28Virginia_State_Route_901-Claiborne_Parkway%2C_Ashburn_Farm%2C_Broadlands%29_in_Broadlands%2C_Loudoun_County%2C_Virginia.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:2018-10-25_14_33_59_View_west_along_Virginia_State_Route_267_(Dulles_Greenway)_at_Exit_5_(Virginia_State_Route_901-Claiborne_Parkway,_Ashburn_Farm,_Broadlands)_in_Broadlands,_Loudoun_County,_Virginia.jpg)

Dulles Greenway Exit 5 on to Claiborne Parkway toward Ashburn Farm

The name Ashburn is believed to have originated from "Ashburn Farm," a 1,236-acre estate originally owned by [John Janney](/wiki/John_Janney "John Janney"), a prominent 19th-century [Quaker lawyer](/wiki/Quakers "Quakers") and politician who served as president of Virginia's Secession Convention in 1861. The property was later purchased by George Lee III in the 1870s, who is thought to have named it "Ashburn" either for the [ash trees](/wiki/Fraxinus "Fraxinus") that dotted the landscape or possibly as a reference to the ash-colored soil or [barn fires](/wiki/Barn_fire "Barn fire") that had previously occurred in the area.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
Originally a quiet farming village known as Farmwell, the area became a part of a larger plantation economy in [Loudoun County](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia"). Ashburn remained largely rural through the early 20th century. It began to change significantly in the 1980s and 1990s with the expansion of the [Dulles Technology Corridor](/wiki/Dulles_Technology_Corridor "Dulles Technology Corridor") and the construction of large neighborhoods such as [Ashburn Village](/wiki/Ashburn_Village "Ashburn Village") and Ashburn Farm.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Today, Ashburn is a central part of [Loudoun County's](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia") high-tech economy and suburban expansion with preserved natural and historic landscapes such as the [W&OD Trail](/wiki/Washington_and_Old_Dominion_Railroad_Regional_Park "Washington and Old Dominion Railroad Regional Park") and nearby farms and battlefields.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

According to the [US Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), Ashburn is a [census designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place"). Despite being restricted to a certain boundary, the broader "Ashburn area" includes adjacent communities and commercial centers that share mailing addresses, infrastructure, and [ZIP Codes](/wiki/ZIP_Code "ZIP Code") like 20147 and 20148. This includes communities such as [Brambleton](/wiki/Brambleton,_Virginia "Brambleton, Virginia"), [Broadlands](/wiki/Broadlands,_Virginia "Broadlands, Virginia"), [Moorefield](/wiki/Moorefield,_Virginia "Moorefield, Virginia"), [Loudoun Valley Estates](/wiki/Loudoun_Valley_Estates,_Virginia "Loudoun Valley Estates, Virginia"), and [Belmont](/wiki/Belmont,_Virginia "Belmont, Virginia"). It originated as farmland and a small rail stop along the [Washington and Old Dominion railroad](/wiki/Washington_and_Old_Dominion_Railroad "Washington and Old Dominion Railroad"), gradually developing into a suburban area as transportation routes and regional growth pushed west from [Washington D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.")[[10]](#cite_note-10)
[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Ashburn, located in eastern [Loudoun County](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia"), Virginia, was originally part of a rural agricultural region known as Farmwell. The land was divided among several estates during the 18th and 19th centuries, forming part of the broader plantation economy that defined much of [northern Virginia](/wiki/Northern_Virginia "Northern Virginia"). During the [Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"), [Loudoun County](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia") was deeply divided, and while Ashburn did not see major battles, it was affected by nearby troop movements, raids, and the broader regional instability at the time.[[12]](#cite_note-ashburn-history-12)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/WASHINGTON_%26_OLD_DOMINION_R.R._CAR_EXTERIOR_LCCN2016861678.jpg/250px-WASHINGTON_%26_OLD_DOMINION_R.R._CAR_EXTERIOR_LCCN2016861678.jpg)](/wiki/File:WASHINGTON_%26_OLD_DOMINION_R.R._CAR_EXTERIOR_LCCN2016861678.jpg)

Exterior of a cart on the Washington and Old Dominion Railroad

In the late 19th century, the arrival of the [Washington and Old Dominion Railroad](/wiki/Washington_and_Old_Dominion_Railroad "Washington and Old Dominion Railroad") provided a transportation link that supported local farms and small industries, although Ashburn itself remained sparsely populated and primarily agricultural well into the 20th century. The [W&OD rail](/wiki/Washington_and_Old_Dominion_Railroad_Regional_Park "Washington and Old Dominion Railroad Regional Park") corridor, now a regional trail, is one of the area's key preserved historical routes. Following the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War"), land ownership changed and farming practices shifted, but Ashburn continued to be defined by its rural character for many decades.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Washington_Dulles_International_Airport_at_Dusk.jpg/250px-Washington_Dulles_International_Airport_at_Dusk.jpg)](/wiki/File:Washington_Dulles_International_Airport_at_Dusk.jpg)

Main Terminal of Washington Dulles International Airport at dusk in Virginia, USA

Significant change began in the second half of the 20th century, particularly after the opening of [Washington Dulles International Airport](/wiki/Dulles_International_Airport "Dulles International Airport") in 1962. This major infrastructure project spurred development throughout the surrounding region, and Ashburn's location made it a strategic site for future suburban and technological expansion. Major development in the 1980s and 1990s that came in the form of suburban neighborhoods and [planned communities](/wiki/Planned_communities "Planned communities") transformed the area into a major residential zone within [Northern Virginia](/wiki/Northern_Virginia "Northern Virginia")'s growing suburban region.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
By the early 2000s, Ashburn had become a hub for global internet infrastructure. A combination of reliable electricity and access to fiber-optic networks attracted major technology firms. The region developed the highest concentration of [data centers](/wiki/Data_center "Data center") in the world, earning the nickname "Data Center Alley." Today, Ashburn is a central component of [Loudoun County's](/wiki/Loudoun_County,_Virginia "Loudoun County, Virginia") tech economy, supporting various technological industries in areas such as [cloud computing](/wiki/Cloud_computing "Cloud computing") and [cybersecurity](/wiki/Cybersecurity "Cybersecurity") while also serving as a key residential area for workers in the greater [Washington, D.C. metro region](/wiki/Washington_metropolitan_area "Washington metropolitan area").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

Ashburn is located in eastern Loudoun County, 7 miles (11 km) southeast of [Leesburg](/wiki/Leesburg,_Virginia "Leesburg, Virginia"), the [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat"), and the same distance north of [Dulles International Airport](/wiki/Dulles_International_Airport "Dulles International Airport"). As drawn by the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau"), the area counted as "Ashburn" extends north to [Virginia State Route 7](/wiki/Virginia_State_Route_7 "Virginia State Route 7"), east to [Virginia State Route 28](/wiki/Virginia_State_Route_28 "Virginia State Route 28"), and southwest to the [Dulles Greenway](/wiki/Dulles_Greenway "Dulles Greenway") (VA 267).[[16]](#cite_note-TIGER-16) The Ashburn CDP is bordered to the north by the [Belmont](/wiki/Belmont,_Virginia "Belmont, Virginia"), [Lansdowne](/wiki/Lansdowne,_Virginia "Lansdowne, Virginia"), [One Loudoun](/wiki/One_Loudoun "One Loudoun"), and [University Center](/wiki/University_Center,_Virginia "University Center, Virginia") CDPs; to the east by the [Kincora](/wiki/Kincora,_Virginia "Kincora, Virginia"), [Dulles Town Center](/wiki/Dulles_Town_Center,_Virginia "Dulles Town Center, Virginia"), and [Sterling](/wiki/Sterling,_Virginia "Sterling, Virginia") CDPs; to the southwest by the [Moorefield](/wiki/Moorefield_Station,_Virginia "Moorefield Station, Virginia") and [Broadlands](/wiki/Broadlands,_Virginia "Broadlands, Virginia") CDPs; and to the west by the [Goose Creek Village](/wiki/Goose_Creek_Village,_Virginia "Goose Creek Village, Virginia") CDP.[[16]](#cite_note-TIGER-16)

According to the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the Ashburn CDP has a total area of 15.55 square miles (40.27 km2), of which 15.30 square miles (39.63 km2) are land and 0.25 square miles (0.65 km2) are water.[[2]](#cite_note-CenPopGazetteer2020-2) The area is drained by [Broad Run](/wiki/Broad_Run_(Loudoun_County,_Virginia) "Broad Run (Loudoun County, Virginia)"), which flows northward through the eastern part of the CDP toward the [Potomac River](/wiki/Potomac_River "Potomac River").

### Subdivisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Subdivisions")]

The Ashburn CDP consists of many major and minor subdivisions such as Ashbrook, Ashburn Farm, [Ashburn Village](/wiki/Ashburn_Village "Ashburn Village"), the Courts and Ridges at Ashburn, and the Village of Waxpool.

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), Ashburn had a population of 46,349. The median age was 39.7 years. 24.5% of residents were under the age of 18 and 15.1% of residents were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 93.2 males, and for every 100 females age 18 and over there were 88.8 males age 18 and over.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-19)[[20]](#cite_note-Census2020PL-20)

100.0% of residents lived in urban areas, while 0.0% lived in rural areas.[[21]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-21)

There were 17,210 households in Ashburn, of which 36.9% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 58.5% were married-couple households, 12.7% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 25.1% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 24.7% of all households were made up of individuals and 12.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-19)

There were 17,906 housing units, of which 3.9% were vacant. The homeowner vacancy rate was 0.4% and the rental vacancy rate was 8.2%.[[19]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-19)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

Ashburn was first listed as a [census designated place](/wiki/Census_designated_place "Census designated place") in the [2010 U.S. census](/wiki/2010_U.S._census "2010 U.S. census").[[18]](#cite_note-2010CensusVA-18)

The [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau") defines Ashburn as a [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP). As of the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census"), the CDP had a population of 43,511 residents,[[23]](#cite_note-Census_2010-23) while the larger [ZIP Code Tabulation Area](/wiki/ZIP_Code_Tabulation_Area "ZIP Code Tabulation Area") (ZCTA) for Ashburn's 20147 ZIP code contained 54,086 people.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

### Other demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other demographics")]

Many of its residents commute into Washington, D.C. and the surrounding suburbs such as [Tysons Corner](/wiki/Tysons_Corner "Tysons Corner") and [Reston](/wiki/Reston,_Virginia "Reston, Virginia") to their places of employment.[[25]](#cite_note-Loudoun_County_Commuter_Bus_Adds_123_Spaces_for_Ashburn_Commuter_Parking-25) The median household income as of 2009 was $100,719.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) The median household size is 2.9 persons.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) 98% of Ashburn residents have a high school degree.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) Some 42 percent of Ashburn's population holds a four-year bachelor's degree;[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) 18 percent holds graduate degrees.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26)

Homeowners formed 80% of the population,[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) renters made up 13% of the population,[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) and 7% of housing units were listed as vacancies.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) The median age of housing was 5.0 years.[[26]](#cite_note-20147_Ashburn_Neighborhood_in_Ashburn,_VA-26) The median housing value is at $630,000.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Economy")]

Located within the [Dulles Technology Corridor](/wiki/Dulles_Technology_Corridor "Dulles Technology Corridor"), Ashburn is home to many high-tech businesses. World Trade Center Dulles Airport is the second [World Trade Center](/wiki/World_Trade_Centers_Association "World Trade Centers Association") in the state.[[28]](#cite_note-WTC_-_Dulles_Airport_at_One_Loudoun-28) [Verizon Business](/wiki/Verizon_Business "Verizon Business") has a major office in Ashburn at the location replacing [MCI WorldCom](/wiki/MCI_WorldCom "MCI WorldCom")'s headquarters after its acquisition.[[29]](#cite_note-Verizon_Business_Global_Llc,_Verizon_Business-29)[[30]](#cite_note-Telecom_Is_Getting_Another_Behemoth-30) Ashburn is also home to government contractor [Telos](/wiki/Telos_(company) "Telos (company)").[[31]](#cite_note-31)

Ashburn is a major hub for [data centers](/wiki/Data_center "Data center"), with companies such as [Digital Realty](/wiki/Digital_Realty "Digital Realty"),[[32]](#cite_note-32) [Equinix](/wiki/Equinix "Equinix"),[[33]](#cite_note-33) and [NTT](/wiki/NTT_Communications "NTT Communications"),[[34]](#cite_note-34) operating multiple facilities there.[[35]](#cite_note-35) Among other websites, the [Wikimedia Foundation](/wiki/Wikimedia_Foundation "Wikimedia Foundation") (parent of Wikipedia) and [Amazon Web Services](/wiki/Amazon_Web_Services "Amazon Web Services") have data centers there.[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[39]](#cite_note-39)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/AWS_servers_in_Ashburn%2C_group_1%2C_2020-10-09_3.jpg/250px-AWS_servers_in_Ashburn%2C_group_1%2C_2020-10-09_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:AWS_servers_in_Ashburn,_group_1,_2020-10-09_3.jpg)

[Amazon Web Services](/wiki/Amazon_Web_Services "Amazon Web Services") data center in Ashburn

The [George Washington University](/wiki/George_Washington_University "George Washington University")'s Virginia Science and Technology Campus and the [Howard Hughes Medical Institute](/wiki/Howard_Hughes_Medical_Institute "Howard Hughes Medical Institute")'s [Janelia Research Campus](/wiki/Janelia_Research_Campus "Janelia Research Campus") are located north of Ashburn in the University Center and Lansdowne CDPs, respectively. The BigBear.ai Performance Center, the training facility for the [Washington Commanders](/wiki/Washington_Commanders "Washington Commanders") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League"), is located in the east part of Ashburn.[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-The_Official_Site_of_the_Washington_Redskins-41)

[Airbus Group, Inc.](/wiki/Airbus_Group,_Inc. "Airbus Group, Inc."), a defense contractor headed by former NASA administrator [Sean O'Keefe](/wiki/Sean_O%27Keefe "Sean O'Keefe"), has a second location in Ashburn in addition to its main office in [Herndon](/wiki/Herndon,_Virginia "Herndon, Virginia"), Virginia.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Education")]

### Colleges and universities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Colleges and universities")]

[George Washington University](/wiki/George_Washington_University "George Washington University") and [Strayer University](/wiki/Strayer_University "Strayer University") have campuses in the area. In December 2009, it was announced that [George Mason University](/wiki/George_Mason_University "George Mason University") was planning to set up a campus in Ashburn, to be located at Exit 6 off the [Dulles Greenway](/wiki/Dulles_Greenway "Dulles Greenway").[[42]](#cite_note-42)

### Primary and secondary schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Primary and secondary schools")]

Main article: [Loudoun County Public Schools](/wiki/Loudoun_County_Public_Schools "Loudoun County Public Schools")

Educational institutions in Ashburn are operated by the [Loudoun County Public Schools](/wiki/Loudoun_County_Public_Schools "Loudoun County Public Schools").

Ashburn's elementary schools include Ashburn Elementary School, Belmont Station Elementary School, Cedar Lane Elementary School, Creighton's Corner Elementary, Discovery Elementary School, Dominion Trail Elementary School, Hillside Elementary School, Legacy Elementary School, Madison's Trust Elementary, Mill Run Elementary School, Moorefield Station Elementary School, Newton-Lee Elementary School, Rosa Lee Carter Elementary School, Sanders Corner Elementary School, Steuart W. Weller Elementary School, and Sycolin Creek Elementary School.

Ashburn's public middle schools include [Eagle Ridge Middle School](/wiki/Loudoun_County_Public_Schools "Loudoun County Public Schools"), [Farmwell Station Middle School](/wiki/Farmwell_Station_Middle_School "Farmwell Station Middle School"), Stone Hill Middle School, [Brambleton Middle School](/wiki/Brambleton_Middle_School "Brambleton Middle School"), Trailside Middle School, and Belmont Ridge Middle School.

Public high schools in Ashburn include [Briar Woods High School](/wiki/Briar_Woods_High_School "Briar Woods High School"), [Broad Run High School](/wiki/Broad_Run_High_School "Broad Run High School"), [Independence High School](/wiki/Independence_High_School_(Ashburn,_Virginia) "Independence High School (Ashburn, Virginia)"), [Rock Ridge High School](/wiki/Rock_Ridge_High_School "Rock Ridge High School"), and [Stone Bridge High School](/wiki/Stone_Bridge_High_School "Stone Bridge High School").[[43]](#cite_note-Loudoun_County_Public_Schools:_Official_Website-43)

There are six private schools in Ashburn: [Loudoun School for Advanced Studies](/wiki/Loudoun_School_for_Advanced_Studies "Loudoun School for Advanced Studies"), St. Theresa Catholic School, Virginia Academy, Leport School, The Loudoun Country Day School, and County Christian School.

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

### Washington Metro

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Washington Metro")]

Ashburn is served by two [Washington Metro](/wiki/Washington_Metro "Washington Metro") [Silver Line](/wiki/Silver_Line_(Washington_Metro) "Silver Line (Washington Metro)") stations. After passing through Dulles Airport, the Silver Line enters Ashburn in the median of the [Dulles Greenway](/wiki/Dulles_Greenway "Dulles Greenway").[[44]](#cite_note-44) The first stop in Ashburn is [Loudoun Gateway station](/wiki/Loudoun_Gateway_station "Loudoun Gateway station"), with [Ashburn station](/wiki/Ashburn_station_(Washington_Metro) "Ashburn station (Washington Metro)") serving as the terminus. Originally planned to open in 2016, the opening was pushed back by multiple delays and finally opened on November 15, 2022.[[45]](#cite_note-newsletter-nov-2012-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)

### Emergency services

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ashburn,_Virginia&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Emergency services")]

Ashburn's fire and [emergency medical services](/wiki/Emergency_medical_services "Emergency medical services") are provided by the [Loudoun County Combined Fire and Rescue System](/wiki/Loudoun_County_Combined_Fire_and_Rescue_System "Loudoun County Combined Fire and Rescue System"). Ashburn Volunteer Fire and Rescue Department (AVFRD) is a volunteer company under LC-CFRS, and serves Ashburn with Stations 6 and 22, which are located near Inova Loudoun Hospital. LC-CFRS career staff operate Moorefield Station 23, near [Ashburn station (Washington Metro)](/wiki/Ashburn_station_(Washington_Metro) "Ashburn station (Washington Metro)"). The Ashburn area is served by the Inova Ashburn Healthplex Emergency Room at the corner of the Dulles Greenway and Loudoun County Parkway as well as Inova Loudoun Hospital, located less than 2 miles (3.2 km) from Ashburn in neighboring [Lansdowne](/wiki/Lansdowne,_Virginia "Lansdowne, Virginia"), and by larger hospitals in the Washington suburbs and city.
